Title: Zaahen
Age: Current4396 – 5996 years old Timeline Born5000 BN – 3400 BN Edit

children.title: Darkin War
children.textContent:
Zaahen was born around 5000 BN - 3400 BN in Shurima. In his adulthood, he became a respected warrior and Ascended into a bird-like form with silver wings. In this time, he selected and directed the Ascensions of many, including: Rhaast, Varus, Xolaani and Setaka. Zaahen swore to fight beside them and guard the light that made them.

While many of his Ascended kin fell to darkness after witnessing the horrors of the Void during the war with Icathia and the collapse of the Shuriman Empire, Zaahen was one of the few Ascended who retained much of their sanity and benevolent nature, despite becoming a Darkin himself. Feeling responsible for their fall from grace, Zaahen fought against his corrupted brethren in the Darkin War, where he and humanity learned how to seal the Darkin into their weapons.

With his new knowledge, Zaahen traveled to the First Lands to aid in the resistance against Darkin invasion, a conflict known now as the "War of the False Kanmei" by Ionians. In Koeshin, he saved the Kinkou Order when they struggled to defend their last stronghold. He met with the leaders of the order: Kennen, The Heart of the Tempest; Yunara, The Fist of Shadow; and Bhinan, The Eye of Twilight; and offered his aid. While wary of allying with one of the god-warriors, the Kinkou had no alternative and accepted it after Bhinan judged his intentions. Zaahen shared how to defeat and seal his siblings and for nearly three years, the Kinkou and Zaahen fought back the Darkin invaders, ultimately sealing Rhaast, 9 Praa, Varus, and Xolaani. While fighting beside him, the Kinkou assigned Yunara to monitor Zaahen and, if necessary, eliminate him should he betray them.

During his confrontation with Rhaast, the two fought for many days and nights, even as their mortal allies rested to count their dead. Zaahen offered Rhaast a chance to fight beside him again—as he had with all his brethren he hunted—but Rhaast, like the others, refused, reveling in his newfound power. He questioned why Zaahen had not joined them, declaring that they were the new masters of this world. As they exchanged blows, Zaahen heard Yunara calling to him. He retorted that they were masters of nothing and that, if the Darkin could not be saved, they had to be destroyed.

After the war ended, Bhinan judged him once more and deemed him safe to return to his homeland. However, Zaahen began to fear that he too would eventually become like his fallen siblings, and asked to be sealed within his glaive in the Temple of Twilight and kept safe within the Spirit Realm. His request was fulfilled, and Yunara stayed with him to protect the Temple for all times.

To help their nation heal, the Kinkou spun his and Yunara's tale into a legend about how a shrine maiden and a mighty spirit fought back against malevolent pretenders in the War of the False Kanmei.

During Zaahen's long confinement within the Spirit Realm, Yunara often visited the temple, though she never crossed its threshold. He believed she wished to free him, but they both knew that she could not. Inside him, a war still raged between the Darkin and the divine. Still, her presence reminded Zaahen of the better part of his nature: his time as an Ascended, when he and his brethren once fought for good. His presence would attract azakana to the temple, but Yunara would repel them.

children.title: Return to Runeterra
children.textContent:
Centuries later, a disturbance in the Spirit Realm sent the Temple of Twilight and Yunara back to the material realm. LeBlanc sought to claim Zaahen's weapon for her own purposes and subjugated the demon Atakhan to locate him.

The Temple of Twilight's return sent the demons of Ionia into a frenzy, prompting them to attack the nearby town of Koeshin, and lured Yunara away from her post. While Yunara and Xin Zhao took up arms to defend the town, Atakhan made his way toward the temple. After defeating the demonic forces, Yunara, Kennen, and Xin were alerted that the temple's defenses had been breached.

As they advanced toward the temple, Yunara and Kennen revealed to Xin the truth about Zaahen. By the time they arrived, however, the temple had already retreated back into the Spirit Realm, with Atakhan already inside. To reach Zaahen, Xin and Yunara would have to overcome the Trials of Twilight, a test created by the temple to judge the strength and honor of those who entered.

Within the temple, Xin Zhao experienced a vision of Demacia in ruins and Jarvan IV corrupted by the Darkin. When presented with a Darkin weapon of his own, Zaahen intervened and warned him not to take it, allowing Xin to pass the trial. Upon reuniting with Yunara, she expressed surprise that Zaahen had aided them and believed there was still good left within him. Refusing to abandon him, they rushed off to find his weapon.

When they finally reached Zaahen's weapon, they found that LeBlanc had already claimed it—now embedded in Atakhan's shadowy flesh. The two tried to fight the demon and free Zaahen, but were overwhelmed by the combined might of the sorceress and her demonic servant. As Atakhan moved in for the kill, Xin reached out to Zaahen's glaive to commune with its spirit. Within Zaahen's mindscape, the fallen god warned Xin to leave, insisting that mortals should not meddle with the power of gods. He declared that he was not the answer to their problems, only evil deferred. But Xin refused, arguing that they had no other choice and that Zaahen's power would darken the world, with or without his permission. Xin reminded him that he had once sacrificed himself to save the world and urged him to let him do the same. Unable to hold back Atakhan's assault anymore, Xin's strength began to fail, and in his final plea, Zaahen finally relented. Taking Xin Zhao as his host, Zaahen returned to the world once more and destroyed Atakhan with a single, devastating slash of his glaive, forcing LeBlanc to retreat, a faint smile on her lips.

After taking Xin Zhao's body, Zaahen quietly departed. Yunara brought Xin's spear home to Demacia, but was unable to tell his people the truth about what befell his body and prayed that Zaahen would honor his sacrifice.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent:
Zaahen is a tall and slender humanoid figure. He has glowing red eyes and a third eye in the center on his forehead, pale gray skin, and long white hair. From the back of his head rise two long, golden horns that curve upward, tapering into darker, teal-tinged tips. He wears no shirt, but his chest and arms are adorned with types of Shuriman amulets and necklaces, and he wears a dark blue cloak around his hips, as well as wings resembling a red cape on his back.

In his Ascended form, Zaahen was a tall humanoid figure with bird-like features. His head is that of a golden falcon with red eyes. He wore robes similar to those of Shuriman priests, in white and gold colors.

When he was human, Zaahen was a brown-skinned man with shaved hair, with a veil on his head, a forehead ornament, and several golden ornaments on his body. He wore robes similar to those of Shuriman priests, in white and gold colors.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent:
Unlike the other Darkin, who have a clear purpose for their carnage, Zaahen stands apart, aware of their race's malicious nature and opposes them. In his free form, he is incredibly stoic, empathetic, and patient, whereas in his glaive form, he seems to become more mysterious.

While many Darkin emerged from their imprisonment consumed by unending torment, Zaahen retains a solemn clarity, suggesting that his experience within the infinite expanse may have differed from that of his kin. Within his mindscape, he appears in his original human form rather than his ascended bird form, symbolizing the enduring identity and humanity he preserves.

Having once guided them as Ascended, Zaahen feels deeply responsible for his siblings' fall from grace. Despite the futility, he offered each Darkin he hunted a chance to fight beside him once more. A piece of his immortal heart breaks each time he has to kill one of his brothers or sisters.

children.title: Darkin
children.textContent: When the Shuriman god-warriors began their conquests throughout Runeterra, Zaahen was saddened to see his kind, the once-noble heroes, fall to the darkness within them. Zaahen felt responsible for the world's suffering and went to slay the Darkin, many of whom he had considered his brothers and sisters. In Ionia, Zaahen helped to seal numerous Darkin into their own weapons, including Rhaast, Varus, and Xolaani. After the war was over, Zaahen chose to be imprisoned in a similar fashion by the Kinkou Order for fear he might one day become like the other Darkin.

children.title: Yunara
children.textContent: Zaahen met Yunara when he came to help defend Ionia from the Darkin and their followers. Zaahen's power impressed the Kinkou, but also made them wary, so they assigned their Fist of Shadow Yunara to keep an eye on him and be ready to eliminate him if he betrayed them. In time, Yunara came to respect Zaahen as a trusted ally and hero of the First Lands, but he had grown to distrust himself and asked the Kinkou to seal him away in the Temple of Twilight, which was then hidden in the spirit realm. Yunara stood guard in the temple for over a thousand years as its protector to ensure Zaahen would not be freed by malevolent forces. When the temple was eventually breached by LeBlanc and Atakhan, Yunara and Xin Zhao went to go stop them, at first believing the Darkin had returned to free Zaahen. After her trial, in which Yunara heard Zaahen's voice, she decided there was still good in Zaahen and went to rescue him from the invaders.

children.title: Xin Zhao
children.textContent: While Xin Zhao first learned of Zaahen from Kennen and Yunara, Zaahen first spoke to the Seneschal in a vision during his trial at the Temple of Twilight, where he sternly warned Xin Zhao that claiming a Darkin weapon was "not the answer" he was seeking, which convinced Xin Zhao and Yunara that there was good in him. When the battle against Atakhan and LeBlanc grew dire, Xin Zhao grabbed the glaive from Atakhan and tried to convince Zaahen to take his body, as the Darkin's power was their only hope for victory. When Zaahen urged Xin Zhao that there was no coming back from his decision, the Seneschal had already decided to sacrifice himself to protect the Darkin's power from falling into the wrong hands. As Xin Zhao's spirit crumbled, Zaahen reluctantly took up his spear and claimed his body to rescue Yunara and eliminate the attackers.

mainContent.textContent:
